title: T-005 — big_loss_categories taxonomy + role→oee.*.* permission seed (OEE §13.3, §3)
prompt:
# T-005 — big_loss_categories + role permission seed

PRD: docs/prd/15-OEE-PRD.md §3 (Personas & Roles), §13.3 (Downtime category colors), §15.4 OEE-M-005 Big Loss Mapping; 02-SETTINGS reference tables registry
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Seed two deterministic data sets used by every downstream OEE task: (a) the canonical Six Big Losses taxonomy with A/P/Q impact dimension and Plant/Process/People Lean class — feeds big_loss_mapping editor (T-021) and Six Big Losses tab (T-018); (b) role→permission mappings for oee_viewer/oee_supervisor/oee_admin per PRD §3 RBAC matrix, referencing strings locked in T-001.

## Pre-flight
Read `packages/db/migrations/` for: latest migration number; T-001 enum strings (must be merged); 02-SETTINGS role_permissions table (existing from 02-SET T-002/T-091). Read PRD §3 RBAC matrix and §13.3 category colors.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_a_big_loss_seed.sql`:
   - CREATE TABLE `big_loss_categories(code TEXT PK, label TEXT, description TEXT, impact_dimension CHAR(1) CHECK (impact_dimension IN ('A','P','Q')), lean_class TEXT CHECK (lean_class IN ('Plant','Process','People')), default_color_hex TEXT, sort_order INT, is_active BOOL DEFAULT TRUE)`. This is a UNIVERSAL reference table (no org_id; cross-tenant taxonomy) — register read-only in 02-SETTINGS reference_tables_registry.
   - INSERT 6 canonical Nakajima TPM rows: `BREAKDOWN/SETUP_ADJ/IDLING/REDUCED_SPEED/STARTUP_REJECT/PRODUCTION_REJECT` mapped to A/A/A/P/Q/Q and Plant/Process/Process/Plant/Process/Process per Nakajima + PRD §13.3 colors.
2. Migration step: seed `role_permissions(role, permission)` rows for oee_viewer/oee_supervisor/oee_admin from PRD §3 matrix; idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Permission strings reference T-001 enum.
3. Drizzle schema `packages/db/src/schema/oee/big-loss-categories.ts` (read-only typed).
4. Vitest: (a) 6 seed rows exist with correct impact_dimension distribution (3xA, 1xP, 2xQ), (b) role_permissions populated for oee_admin (all 13 strings), oee_supervisor (read+annotate+escalate+ack subset), oee_viewer (read+export), (c) seed is idempotent (re-run yields same row count).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_a_big_loss_seed.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/oee/big-loss-categories.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/oee-a-big-loss-seed.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration runs on a fresh DB, when `SELECT count(*), impact_dimension FROM big_loss_categories GROUP BY impact_dimension` runs, then 3 rows A, 1 row P, 2 rows Q (total 6).
2. Given migration runs, when `SELECT permission FROM role_permissions WHERE role='oee_admin'` runs, then all 13 oee.*.* strings from T-001 are returned.
3. Given migration is re-applied on a populated DB, when complete, then row counts in big_loss_categories and role_permissions(oee_*) are unchanged (idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
4. Given 02-SETTINGS reference_tables_registry is queried, when filtered for big_loss_categories, then it appears with read-only flag.

## Test strategy
- RED: vitest oee-a-big-loss-seed asserting four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test oee-a-big-loss-seed`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not change Nakajima TPM canonical 6-category names — they are an industry standard referenced by §14 regulatory alignment + §10.3 dashboards.
- Do not org-scope big_loss_categories — taxonomy is UNIVERSAL per ADR-034.
- Do not skip ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — migrations re-apply during CI tests.
- Do not hard-code role names elsewhere; rely on 02-SETTINGS roles already seeded.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed big_loss_categories Nakajima taxonomy + role→oee.*.* permission mappings per PRD §3 RBAC matrix. Idempotent.
pipeline_inputs.details: UNIVERSAL taxonomy (no org_id). Permission seed binds oee_admin to all 13 strings; oee_supervisor to read+annotate+escalate+ack subset; oee_viewer to read+export. Big Loss editor (T-021) writes tenant overrides via OEE-M-005 mapping table (created in T-021 if needed). Registry insert lets 02-SETTINGS reference-tables viewer surface big_loss_categories alongside shift_configs/oee_alert_thresholds.
